    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (counting numbers ≤ x that are sums of two squarefull numbers; asymptotic formula).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er76e]` and `[Od81]`.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1081 ... end Erdos1081`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1081`.
    25→- [x] `open Filter Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.
    26→

    29→- [x] `IsSquarefull` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30→- [x] `IsSumOfTwoSquarefull` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    31→- [x] `countA` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    32→- [x] `countA` is marked `noncomputable` (correct — `Finset.filter` on a `Prop` using `open Classical` requires it).
    33→- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    34→- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSquarefull`, `IsSumOfTwoSquarefull`.
    35→- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `countA`.
    36→- [x] No local notation needed — N/A.
    37→- [x] No `@[category test/API]` sanity checks present (optional; not required).
    38→
    39→## 6. Theorem Statements
    40→
    41→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    42→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1081` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
    43→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
    44→- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) explaining the problem and noting it was disproved.
    45→- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er76e]` and `[Od81]`.
    46→
    47→### `answer()` usage
    48→
    49→The problem asks "Is it true that A(x) ~ c·x/√(log x) for some c > 0?" — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    50→
    51→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P` (Case 1 form).
    52→- [x] `answer(False)` is used because the problem was **disproved** by Odoni [Od81] — the answer to the yes/no question is "no".
    53→- [x] The RHS (`∃ c : ℝ, c > 0 ∧ Tendsto ...`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no".
    54→- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ c : ℝ, ...`) appear **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.
    55→
    56→## 7. Category Attribute
    57→
    58→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    59→- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid and appropriate (Odoni disproved the conjecture).
    60→- [x] Not marked `research open` (correctly, since it was solved/disproved).
    61→

    63→
    64→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 11`.
    65→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
    66→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
    67→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    68→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.
    69→

    73→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    74→- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
    75→- [x] No `native_decide`.

    78→
    79→- N/A — no variants present. Single-part problem.
    80→
    81→## 11. Code Quality
    82→
    83→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    84→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `∣`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`.
    85→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    86→- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1081.lean` — Erdős Problem 1081

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1081`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1081`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (counting numbers ≤ x that are sums of two squarefull numbers; asymptotic formula).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er76e]` and `[Od81]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1081 ... end Erdos1081`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1081`.
- [x] `open Filter Classical` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsSquarefull` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `IsSumOfTwoSquarefull` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `countA` has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `countA` is marked `noncomputable` (correct — `Finset.filter` on a `Prop` using `open Classical` requires it).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSquarefull`, `IsSumOfTwoSquarefull`.
- [x] Function returning a value uses `lowerCamelCase`: `countA`.
- [x] No local notation needed — N/A.
- [x] No `@[category test/API]` sanity checks present (optional; not required).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1081` (snake_case, matches namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) explaining the problem and noting it was disproved.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er76e]` and `[Od81]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Is it true that A(x) ~ c·x/√(log x) for some c > 0?" — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P` (Case 1 form).
- [x] `answer(False)` is used because the problem was **disproved** by Odoni [Od81] — the answer to the yes/no question is "no".
- [x] The RHS (`∃ c : ℝ, c > 0 ∧ Tendsto ...`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ c : ℝ, ...`) appear **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — valid and appropriate (Odoni disproved the conjecture).
- [x] Not marked `research open` (correctly, since it was solved/disproved).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11` (Number theory).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` form (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. Single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `∣`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review (requires build environment).

---

**Verdict:** PASS — all statically-verifiable checklist items are satisfied. The file is clean, well-structured, and correctly uses `answer(False)` for a disproved yes/no conjecture. Build verification is pending.

content.text: The review passed. No changes needed.
